channel.title: MSU Sports Analytics Certificate
channel.description: Recent content on MSU Sports Analytics Certificate

item.title: 2025-2026 Class
item.description: Ryan Bernardo Associate Director, Revenue Generation & Data Analytics, University of Florida Capstone Project: To Be Announced 2025-2026 Haeyong Chun, PhD Department of Kinesiology, Michigan State University Capstone Project: To Be Announced 2025-2026 Katherine Curtis PhD Candidate, Department of Kinesiology, Michigan State University Capstone Project: To Be Announced 2025-2026 Archer Guanco Head of Data Analysis, Detroit City FC Capstone Project: To Be Announced 2025-2026 Joey Larabee Data Science Masters Student, Michigan State University Capstone Project: To Be Announced 2025-2026 Joshua Vicente PhD Candidate, Department of Kinesiology, Michigan State University Capstone Project: To Be Announced 2025-2026

item.title: In the Media
item.description: Our instructors and students share insights about sports analytics, career transitions, and the future of data in sports through podcasts, videos, and interviews.

item.title: Instructors
item.description: Our instructors aren’t just academics—they’re professionals who pivoted from STEM research and professional practice into sports analytics and higher education research. Albert Cohen PhD Mathematics, Carnegie Mellon University Courses: MTH 801 (Fall 2023, Fall 2024, Fall 2025) Emilio Xavier Esposito PhD Computational Chemistry, Duquesne University Courses: STT 834 (Spring 2024, Spring 2026), MTH 803 (Spring 2025, Spring 2026) Leonard C Johnson PhD Statistics, Western Michigan University Courses: STT 832 (Fall 2023, Fall 2024, Fall 2025) Erin Neaton PhD Psychology, Michigan State University Courses: STT 834 (Spring 2024) Trevor Nill BS Mechanical Engineering, Michigan State University Courses: MTH 803 (Spring 2024)

item.title: MTH 801: Machine Learning Algorithms
item.description: The program’s foundational course builds the mathematical and computational scaffolding students need to engage seriously with sports analytics. Working through regression, classification, and dimensionality reduction methods, students develop both theoretical understanding and hands-on experience applying these tools to sports-related questions in R. The emphasis is on understanding why methods work, not just executing them — a distinction that pays dividends in every subsequent course.

item.title: Program Overview
item.description: The Sports Analytics Certificate bridges academic training and professional practice through four strategically sequenced courses. You’ll build machine learning foundations, master R programming and visualization, engage with industry decision-makers, and complete a public-facing capstone project. What Makes Us Different Student-First Education: Courses are tailored to bring all students to the same level of excellence Integrated Capstone Design: MTH 803 and STT 834 run together, giving you months (not weeks) to develop meaningful projects GitHub-First Approach: Your project website becomes your portfolio, not just a course deliverable Practitioner-Driven Learning: Weekly standups mirror industry workflow; guest speakers share real implementation challenges AI Partnership Skills: Explicit guidance on using AI tools responsibly—debugging, exploring approaches, and maintaining understanding Program Format 100% online, synchronous Certificate completion: 8 months (minimum duration) Small cohorts (6-13 students) enable meaningful collaboration MTH 801 Machine Learning Algorithms Fall Mathematical foundations of ML methods—regression, PCA, SVMs, and neural networks—applied to sports questions in R.

item.title: 2024-2025 Alumni
item.description: Zach Maxey Implementation Analyst, Banyan Technology Artificial Intelligence Consultant, Maxey Industries Capstone Project: Stuff+ Model 2024-2025 River Melcher Onsite Specialist, Stryker Capstone Project: Analyzing the Effect of Acute Workload Increases on Non-Contact Injury in Division I Football Players 2024-2025

item.title: Data Resources
item.description: A curated starting point for finding sports data. Whether you’re building a class project or exploring a research question, these sources cover the major North American sports leagues and beyond. Public Data & Reference Sites Free, browser-accessible statistics and historical data — no API key required. Sports Reference — A robust collection of data across different sports Baseball Reference — comprehensive MLB statistics, historical records, and WAR calculations Pro Football Reference — NFL statistics, draft history, and advanced metrics Basketball Reference — NBA/WNBA statistics, shot logs, and play-by-play data Hockey Reference — NHL statistics and historical records FBref — soccer statistics across major global leagues (powered by StatsBomb) Sports Reference College Sports — college football, basketball, and baseball data SportsDataVerse — college football, basketball, and baseball data APIs & Data Providers Programmatic access to sports data, ranging from free tiers to professional subscriptions.

item.title: STT 832: Statistical Learning and Data Mining
item.description: Students develop fluency in R as an analytical environment, with particular attention to transforming raw data into clear, compelling visualizations that can inform decisions. The course covers data cleaning, exploration, and the construction of publication-quality graphics, building the kind of practical programming confidence that separates analysts who can execute from those who can only supervise. By the end, students are equipped to wrangle messy real-world data and tell coherent stories with what they find.

item.title: 2023-2024 Alumni
item.description: Corey Grozier, PhD Assistant Professor, Department of Kinesiology & Sport Management, Texas Tech University, Lubbock Capstone Project: The Value of Defense in the NHL 2023-2024 Arjun Parmar, PhD Sport Scientist, Dallas Stars Capstone Project: Pitching Mechanics 2023-2024 Scott Smoot Sports Science Coordinator, Football, Rutgers University Capstone Project: Changing College Basketball Conferences: Impact on Player Performance 2023-2024 Johnny Vecchio Analyst, McQueen Financial Advisors Assistant Baseball Coach, Ernest W. Seaholm High School Capstone Project: Baseball Shift Past-and-Present 2023-2024 Nate Wellman Administrative Business Analyst, Michigan State University Capstone Project: Financial Modeling of Football (Soccer) Contracts 2023-2024

item.title: Guest Speakers
item.description: Our guest speakers are practitioners working across teams, leagues, agencies, and media; sharing how analytics actually gets used (and sometimes ignored) in real organizations. Participation by guest speakers does not imply endorsement of MSU’s Sports Analytics Graduate Certificate. Brian Lucena, PhD Principal, Numeristical Reproducible Models and Sports Betting Spring 2024 Amelia Probst Data Scientist, Demand Analytics Wrangling and Using NFL Data and Career Paths Spring 2024 Ticket Pricing Spring 2026 Chris Fonnesbeck Principal Quantitative Analyst, Philadelphia Phillies Wrangling and Using NFL Data and Career Paths Spring 2024 Sarah Rudd Co-founder and CEO, src | ftbl Football (Soccer) Data and Career Paths Spring 2024 Nickolas Gatt Research & Development Analyst, National Hockey League (NHL) Collecting, Cleaning, and Using NHL Data and Career Paths Spring 2024 Michael Ristich, PhD Academic Specialist, Writing, Rhetoric, and Cultures, Michigan State University Using Generative AI in Research

item.title: MTH 803: Sports Analytics Practicum
item.description: Rather than adding more methods to the toolbox, this course asks students to engage directly with the people using analytics inside sports organizations. Through a structured guest speaker series spanning team operations, league offices, player representation, and sports media, students practice the art of strategic inquiry — learning to ask questions that reveal how decisions actually get made, not just how they’re supposed to. The conversational format doubles as professional networking experience, and the relationships students build here often extend well beyond the semester.

item.title: Sports Analytics Organizations
item.description: Getting involved in the sports analytics community accelerates career growth, keeps you current with the field, and builds the network that opens doors. These are the organizations and events worth knowing. Professional Organizations Society for American Baseball Research (SABR) — the original sports analytics community; home of the Analytics Conference each spring and the Analytics chapter. Membership is open to all levels. Sports Analytics Association (SAA) — practitioner-focused community hosting webinars, job resources, and the annual Sports Analytics World Series conference Harvard Sports Analysis Collective (HSAC) — student-run organization producing original research; a model for building an analytics culture in academic settings Analytics at Wharton — hosts the Wharton Sports Analytics and Business Initiative (WSABI) and publishes practitioner-focused research Sigma (formerly HBSA) — Hockey analytics community with an annual conference and a welcoming entry point for hockey-specific work Flagship Conferences MIT Sloan Sports Analytics Conference (SSAC) — the highest-profile annual conference in sports analytics; held each spring in Boston.

item.title: Code of Conduct
item.description: The MSU Sports Analytics Certificate adopts the Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct v3.0 to ensure our learning environment supports all students regardless of background, identity, or experience level. Our Pledge We pledge to make our community welcoming, safe, and equitable for all. We are committed to fostering an environment that respects and promotes the dignity, rights, and contributions of all individuals, regardless of characteristics including race, ethnicity, caste, color, age, physical characteristics, neurodiversity, disability, sex or gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, language, philosophy or religion, national or social origin, socio-economic position, level of education, or other status.

item.title: STT 834: Sports Analytics Capstone
item.description: The capstone is where everything comes together: students independently scope, execute, and present a portfolio-quality analytics project addressing a real personnel or revenue decision from an organizational perspective. Deliverables go beyond a written report to include a documented GitHub repository and a professional project website built with GitHub Pages — the kinds of artifacts that actually move the needle in a job search. Weekly standups, peer critique, and individual instructor support keep projects on track while building the collaborative habits that define effective analytics teams.

item.title: Mentoring Guide
item.description: Adapted from “How I Like to be Mentored” by Emilio Xavier Esposito. Used with permission. The most successful learning relationships aren’t just technically proficient — they’re built on foundations of mutual understanding and intentional mentorship. Misaligned expectations and communication styles create unnecessary friction in otherwise talented teams. This guide helps you address that up front. By investing 30 minutes in structured self-reflection, you can prevent miscommunication and accelerate your growth in the program.

item.title: Program Administration
item.description: Albert Cohen, PhD Program Director Bill Burghardt, PhD Program Director Alyssa Suzanne Garber Administrative Support
